# Code of Conduct

## Contributor Covenant

This project follows the [Contributor Covenant v2.1](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/). All contributors, maintainers, and community members are expected to uphold these standards.

### Our Pledge

We pledge to make participation in our community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, visible or invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity and expression, level of experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, or sexual identity and orientation.

### Our Standards

**Positive behavior:**
- Demonstrating empathy and kindness
- Being respectful of differing viewpoints and experiences
- Giving and accepting constructive feedback gracefully
- Focusing on what is best for the community
- Accepting responsibility and apologizing for mistakes

**Unacceptable behavior:**
- Sexualized language or imagery, or unwelcome sexual attention
- Trolling, insulting/derogatory comments, personal or political attacks
- Public or private harassment
- Publishing others' private information without explicit permission
- Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate

### Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be reported to the project maintainers. All complaints will be reviewed and investigated promptly and fairly.

## Fury Auditor Code of Conduct

Fury auditors occupy a position of trust in the Styx ecosystem. In addition to the Contributor Covenant above, auditors must adhere to:

### Integrity

- **Honest verdicts**: Judge proof submissions based solely on the evidence presented. Never rubber-stamp or reflexively reject.
- **No collusion**: Do not coordinate verdicts with other auditors. The routing system ensures anonymity for a reason.
- **Conflict disclosure**: If you recognize a submitter (despite anonymization), recuse yourself from that review.

### Confidentiality

- **Proof privacy**: Proof media (photos, videos, sensor data) is confidential. Do not screenshot, download, share, or discuss specific proof content outside the platform.
- **Anonymity**: Do not attempt to identify other auditors or submitters beyond what the platform reveals.

### Accountability

- **Stake responsibility**: Your auditor stake ($2.00 per audit) reflects your commitment to accurate judgment. False accusations carry a 3x penalty to your accuracy score.
- **Burn-in period**: Your first 10 audits are a calibration period. Maintain > 80% accuracy after burn-in or face demotion.
- **Appeals**: If your verdict is overturned, review the appeal reasoning to calibrate future judgments. Repeated overturns trigger review.

### Recovery Contract Sensitivity

- **No-contact contracts**: These involve personal boundaries and potentially vulnerable situations. Apply extra care and empathy when reviewing compliance evidence.
- **Health contracts**: BMI floor (18.5) and velocity caps (2% weekly loss) exist for safety. If you observe evidence suggesting a user may be in danger, flag it for platform review rather than issuing a simple pass/fail.
